title: Imaging single AF647 molecules immobilised in PVA on a cover glass
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e5flm","text":"This is a protocol for the preparation of a microscopy sample of single Alexa Fluor 647 molecules immobilised in PVA polymer on a cover glass. This protocol was used to generate the data shown in Figure 2i and 1j of the following publication:\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":196,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fpps2","text":"Bruggeman et al., POLCAM: Instant molecular orientation microscopy for the life sciences. bioRxiv 2023.02.07.527479 (Feb 2023), doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2023.02.07.527479","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":133,"length":41}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"D18236A7AEFF11EEAB870A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://doi.org/10.1101/2023.02.07.527479"}}}}
steps: Argon plasma clean cover glass (VWR collection, 631-0124) for 00:30:00 in a plasma cleaner (Expanded Plasma Cleaner, PDC-002, Harrick Plasma).
Dilute Alexa Fluor 647 (AF647) to 500 picomolar (pM) in the filtered 1% PVA solution.
Dissolve 1 g of solid PVA into 100 mL of filtered milli-Q water in a glass beaker.
Filter 100 mL Milli-Q water using a syringe filter (0.02 μm syringe filter, Whatman, 6809-1102).

Let the solution cool. Once cooled, filter it using a syringe filter (0.02 μm syringe filter, Whatman, 6809-1102). You can store the filtered 1% PVA solution at 4 °C for later use. The filter tends to clog if you try to filter too large a volume of 1% PVA in one go. If this happens, filter in separate smaller batches, replacing the filter each time for a new one.

Place a clean cover glass into a spincoater (WS-650Mz-23NPPB, Laurell) and spin-cast 10 µL of the AF647 solution onto the cover glass for 00:00:45 at 3000 rpm, , . To avoid forgetting which side of the cover glass the sample was put on, you can use a marker to place a small dot in the corner of the cover glass before spin coating.

Optional : Seal the sample by sticking it to a standard glass microscope slide (631-1550, VWR) using a double-sided frame-seal slide chamber sticker (9x9 mm, SLF0201, Bio-rad). Make sure the coated side of the cover glass is facing the microscope slide.
Place the beaker on a stirring hot plate and heat the solution to 90 °C under continuous stirring for 00:30:00 or until the PVA is completely dissolved.
creator: Ezra Bruggeman
